slug: 防晒霜是怎么挡住看不见的光的
title: 防晒霜是怎么挡住看不见的光的

outline.concept: UV photons carry more energy per photon than visible light — enough to snap chemical bonds in your DNA. Sunburn isn't from heat; it's from invisible photons doing molecular-level demolition.
outline.query: 阴天晒伤 没感觉热 紫外线 看不见 晒红

outline.concept: Physical sunscreens (zinc oxide, titanium dioxide) are nano-scale mirrors: their particles scatter and reflect UV photons away from skin. Same stuff as white paint pigment, just ground small enough to not look chalky — mostly.
outline.query: 防晒霜泛白 涂完发白 物理防晒 氧化锌 白泥

outline.concept: Chemical sunscreens are molecular shock absorbers: their conjugated π-electron systems absorb a UV photon, an electron jumps to an excited state, then the molecule relaxes by dumping that energy as tiny heat vibrations — the photon never reaches your skin cells.
outline.query: 涂防晒发热 化学防晒 吸收 转化 分子 电子跃迁

outline.concept: One molecule can't cover all UV wavelengths. UVB (280-320nm, burning) and UVA (320-400nm, aging/penetrates deeper) need different absorbers. Broad-spectrum sunscreen is literally a cocktail of molecules, each covering a different UV band.
outline.query: UVA UVB 广谱防晒 抗老 选防晒 SPF PA
